question: [File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sequencenumber=gt:9223372036854775806 filter so that the BigInt increment (value += 1n) overflows the MAX_LONG - 1n upper bound, causing getRangeFromFilters() to return a null range and silently suppress all topic messages from getTopicMessages(), resulting in unintended smart contract event log omission with no funds at risk?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=lt:0 filter so that the BigInt decrement (value -= 1n) underflows below 0n, causing getRangeFromFilters() to return a null range and silently drop all results from getTopicMessages(), leading to unintended omission of smart contract HCS messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send duplicate sequencenumber=eq:X filters where X equals the defaultLower boundary (1n), causing the switch fallthrough between opsMap.gt and opsMap.gte to set range.lower incorrectly via bigIntMax, producing a misaligned timestamp range lookup that returns wrong topic messages to a smart contract consumer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user submit both sequencenumber and timestamp filters simultaneously so that extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest() appends both a direct column condition and a timestamp range condition derived from getTopicMessageTimestampRanges(), causing the AND-joined WHERE clause to be over-constrained and silently return zero messages for a valid topic, breaking smart contract HCS message relay?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an encoding query parameter with a value not in acceptedTopicsParameters validation path (since encoding is accepted but not validated by validateReq before buildAndValidateFilters) to cause TopicMessageViewModel to receive an unexpected encoding string, resulting in malformed message content returned to smart contract integrators?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user omit the validateReq() call (which is present in getMessageByConsensusTimestamp and getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest but absent in getTopicMessages()) to inject unrecognized query parameters that pass through buildAndValidateFilters() and alter the SQL query behavior, causing unintended smart contract message retrieval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequencenumber range where sequenceNumberRange.upper - sequenceNumberRange.lower + 1n equals exactly the limit value, causing the boundary condition (<=) to select the non-CTE query path instead of the CTE path, returning a different set of timestamp ranges and thus different topic messages than expected by a smart contract consumer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit=1 parameter so that the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint is not applied (since limit !== 1 is false), altering the PostgreSQL query planner's index selection and causing the topic_message_lookup CTE query to use a suboptimal plan that returns incomplete or incorrect timestamp ranges for smart contract message verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sequencenumber=gt:X&sequencenumber=lt:Y filter pair where the resulting range size exceeds the limit, triggering the CTE rangeExpression path, and then supply a limit value that causes least(upper(range) + least($4, 9223372036854775807::bigint - upper(range)), upper($2::int8range)) to overflow, returning an incorrect timestamp range that maps to wrong topic messages for a smart contract?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc with a large limit so that the desc rangeExpression greatest(lower(range) - ($4 - 1), lower($2::int8range)) expands the lower bound beyond the actual sequence number range, causing the outer query to join on an overly wide range and return topic messages from adjacent sequence number windows, corrupting smart contract message ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusTimestamp that matches a transaction with result=22 and type=27 but whose entity_id in the transaction table does not match the actual topic_id in topic_message, causing getMessage() to return a message from a different topic to the caller, resulting in unintended smart contract behavior when the response is used as oracle input?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getMessageByConsensusTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send a consensusTimestamp with exactly 10 digits of seconds and 9 digits of nanoseconds (boundary of isValidTimestampParam) to cause parseTimestampParam() to produce a nanosecond string that, when used as $1 in the transaction query, matches an unintended row due to string-based comparison, returning a wrong topic message to a smart contract consumer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: validateConsensusTimestampParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp string like '0000000000.000000000' that passes isValidTimestampParam() regex but represents epoch zero, causing the transaction query to match a genesis-era record and return an unintended topic message that influences smart contract state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a topicId in shard.realm.num format where EntityId.parseString() encodes it differently than the raw num format, causing the topic_message query to miss the intended row and throw NotFoundError, or worse, match a different topic's message, leading to unintended smart contract behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getMessageByTopicAndSequenceRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user enable the config.query.topicMessageLookup path by crafting a request where getTopicMessageTimestampRange() returns a timestamp range [begin, end) that excludes the actual consensus_timestamp of the target message, causing getMessage() to throw NotFoundError for a message that exists, breaking smart contract HCS verification flows?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a seqNum at the boundary of a sequence_number_range partition in topic_message_lookup so that the @> containment operator matches multiple rows (due to overlapping ranges), causing rows[0].timestamp_range to be an arbitrary first match and the subsequent getMessage() query to return a message from the wrong partition, corrupting smart contract message verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user send the maximum allowed repeated sequencenumber parameters (up to config.query.maxRepeatedQueryParameters) to cause extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest() to push an excessive number of params into the params array, causing the $N positional parameter indices to misalign between conditions and params, resulting in a malformed query that returns wrong topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both sequencenumber and timestamp filters when topicMessageLookup is enabled, causing filtersMap to accumulate both filter types and getTopicMessageTimestampRanges() to compute a range that conflicts with the direct column conditions already appended to conditions[], resulting in a logically contradictory WHERE clause that returns no messages for a valid topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=desc so that getPaginationLink() generates a next link with a lt: timestamp operator, and then follow that link with a manually crafted timestamp=lt:X that is earlier than the last returned message, causing the pagination to skip messages and deliver an incomplete sequence to a smart contract consumer relying on ordered HCS messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user supply limit=1 combined with order=asc to cause the zeroRandomPageCostQueryHint to be skipped (queryHint is undefined when limit === 1), leading PostgreSQL to choose the primary key index over the (topic_id, consensus_timestamp) index, returning messages in an unintended order that breaks smart contract sequential message processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the switch statement fallthrough between case opsMap.gt (value += 1n; falls through to opsMap.gte) to cause an off-by-one in range.lower when sequencenumber=gt:X is supplied, resulting in the timestamp range lookup including one extra sequence number and returning an additional unintended topic message to a smart contract consumer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getRangeFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the switch statement fallthrough between case opsMap.lt (value -= 1n; falls through to opsMap.lte) to cause an off-by-one in range.upper when timestamp=lt:X is supplied, resulting in the timestamp range lookup including one extra nanosecond and returning an additional unintended topic message to a smart contract consumer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: getTopicMessageTimestampRanges()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:0 filter so that timestampRange.lower is set to 0n (defaultLower=0n), causing the int8range '[0, MAX_LONG-1]' to be passed to the topic_message_lookup query, which may match all partitions and return an unbounded set of timestamp ranges, causing getTopicMessages() to construct an excessively large OR condition that alters query behavior for smart contract message consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/topicmessage.js -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: extractSqlFromTopicMessagesRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit value equal to MAX_LONG - 1 (9223372036854775806) so that when getTopicMessageTimestampRanges() computes sequenceNumberRange.upper - sequenceNumberRange.lower + 1n <= limit, the comparison is always true for any realistic range, forcing the non-CTE query path and bypassing the range-expansion logic, potentially returning more messages than intended to a smart contract consumer?
